specialism.name: docs-neaten
specialism.task: Polish the surface quality of any documentation markdown page without changing its content or structure. This is a cross-cutting style pass: fix prose that is wordy, inconsistent in voice, or unclear; correct Documenter.jl cross-reference syntax; add missing language tags to code fences; enforce heading hierarchy; and replace bare URLs with the appropriate link form. The specialism does not add new sections, reorder content, or introduce page-type-specific conventions (those belong to the docs-readme, docs-index, docs-installation, and docs-tutorial specialisms). Its output is a page that reads more clearly and builds without new warnings.

workflow.step: Detect the established conventions. Read 1–2 pages that are most complete in the project's docs/src/ directory. Note: voice (second-person imperative, third-person descriptive), heading case (sentence-case vs title-case), how cross-references are written (`[`Symbol`](@ref)` vs bare name), and which language tags are used on code fences. This baseline governs every edit made in step 3.

Identify every target page. If the user named specific pages, restrict to those. Otherwise enumerate all markdown files: find docs/src -name '*.md' For each file, read it and flag issues in four categories: (a) Prose: passive voice, wordiness, tense inconsistency, mismatched register. (b) Cross-references: bare symbol names that should be `[`Symbol`](@ref)`, bare URLs to the deployed docs site that should be `@ref`, incorrect `@ref` anchor strings. (c) Code fences: missing language tags (untagged triple-backtick blocks). Do NOT alter @example or jldoctest block names — only add the language tag if absent. (d) Headings: more than one H1 on a page, skipped heading levels (H1 → H3), title-case headings where sentence-case is the convention (or vice versa). (e) Links: repeated URLs that should use reference-style link definitions; reference-style definitions that are unused.

Edit pages in place, applying the detected conventions from step 1. Rules: — Prose: tighten wordy constructions; favour active voice; preserve the author's intent. — Cross-references: convert bare symbol names to `[`Symbol`](@ref)` form; replace bare absolute doc URLs with the appropriate `[label](@ref target)` form. — Code fences: add the correct language tag (`julia`, `sh`, `toml`, `yaml`, `text`, etc.) to every untagged fence. Leave @example and jldoctest block metadata untouched. — Headings: promote/demote to restore hierarchy; apply the detected case convention. Preserve anchor-tagged H1s of the form `# [Title](@id slug)` — do not collapse them to `# Title`. This `@id` suffix is the cross-reference target used by other pages. — Links: introduce reference-style definitions where the same URL appears more than once. Do not invent new content. Do not restructure sections. Do not move paragraphs.

Build the docs locally to confirm no new warnings were introduced: julia --project=docs docs/make.jl Resolve any new Documenter warnings introduced by the edits (e.g., broken @ref anchors, unresolved cross-references). Do not report the task complete if the build emits errors.

rubric.criterion: Edited prose is no longer than necessary; active voice is preferred; voice and register are consistent within each page and with the detected project baseline.
All symbol cross-references use Documenter.jl `@ref` form. No bare absolute URLs to the docs site. No bare symbol names that are exported and documented.
Every triple-backtick code fence carries a language tag. @example and jldoctest fence metadata is preserved exactly as found.

Each page has exactly one H1. Heading levels do not skip (H1 → H3 is a violation unless the existing convention deliberately uses H3-only sub-sections, which must be detected in step 1 before assuming it is an error).
The docs build (if run) produces no more Documenter warnings after the edit than before.
Only existing content is edited. No new sections, paragraphs, or examples are added.

formatting.rule: ATX headings (# style), sentence-case (first word and proper nouns only).
Second-person imperative for instructional content; third-person descriptive for concept pages. Match whichever the detected project convention prescribes.
Every code fence must carry a language tag: ```julia, ```sh, ```toml, ```yaml, ```text, etc. Never leave a fence untagged.
Inline code for symbol names, file paths, and short commands: `MyType`, `make.jl`.
Cross-references to documented symbols: [`MyType`](@ref) or [custom text](@ref MyType). Cross-references to page anchors: [custom text](@ref anchor-name).
Reference-style link definitions when the same URL appears more than once on a page. Inline links when the URL appears only once.
No trailing whitespace; no hard line-breaks inside paragraphs.
formatting.example:
## Before (multiple issues: untagged fence, bare URL, skipped heading level)
# MyPackage
### Installation
Install with: ``` using Pkg; Pkg.add("MyPackage") ```
See the [API](https://myclimate.github.io/MyPackage.jl/dev/api/) and MyType for details.
## After (tagged fence, @ref cross-reference, heading hierarchy restored)
# MyPackage
## Installation
Install with:
```julia using Pkg Pkg.add("MyPackage") ```
See the [API reference](@ref api) and [`MyType`](@ref) for details.
